items.source: Absence across all Salesforce fields and Next Step

sources: SF Next_Step__c (full history Nov 2025 to Jun 2026): no mention of quantified pain or ROI
Products: iContract + Merlin (scope known)
Gap: no Salesforce fields capture the business problem or value case
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or value case is documented in Salesforce or Next Step. The deal history shows a detailed RFP response (Nov 2025), on-site demo (2 Mar 2026), and BAFO pricing (24 Mar 2026), but the buyer's stated pain, cost savings target, cycle-time reduction, or ROI has never been recorded. Without a quantified value case, it is difficult to defend the $100k ACV if the other finalist undercuts us on price. The partner (BCT) led the demo and may hold the business-case knowledge, but Zycus does not. At 7 days to close with an unmapped Economic Buyer and an unknown competitor, the absence of a documented metrics-driven justification is a critical gap. The next move is to confirm with the partner what quantified value was presented to the buyer and whether the buyer accepted it as the basis for the investment.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: Mariana mentioned 15 Jun and 24 Jun (coordinating decision timeline)
SF Next_Step__c: BCT partner led demo, on-site, MSA, reference call (active Nov 2025 to May 2026)
SF OpportunityContactRole: no Champion role logged
Gap: no internal IDB champion identified; BCT is external partner, not internal advocate
champion.narrative: There is no documented internal champion at IDB Invest. Mariana is the only named buyer contact (Next Step 15 Jun, 24 Jun), and she is coordinating the decision timeline, but her role (procurement lead, sourcing manager, functional buyer) and her advocacy for Zycus are unknown. She has not been logged as a contact role. The partner, BCT, is the true champion for this deal: BCT led the demo (Jan 2026), coordinated the on-site session (2 Mar), aligned the MSA (April), and coordinated the PwC reference call (18 May). BCT's engagement has been active and consistent throughout the cycle (Next Step entries Nov 2025 to May 2026 reference BCT at every stage). However, BCT is an external implementation partner, not an internal buyer champion. At 7 days to close, the absence of a named internal champion with access to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker is a critical risk. If Mariana or another internal stakeholder is championing Zycus, their advocacy has not been made visible to the deal team. The next move is to ask the partner (BCT) to name the internal champion, confirm their support, and assess whether they have the influence to close the deal if the competitor puts forward a lower price or a competing narrative.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: IDB notified Zycus as 1 of 2 finalists, 11 May 2026
SF Competitors__c: unknown
Gap: competitor name not documented; cannot position differentiation or defend pricing
competition.narrative: Zycus is competing blind. IDB Invest confirmed Zycus as 1 of 2 finalists on 11 May 2026 (Next Step 11 May), but the other finalist's name is not documented in Salesforce or Next Step. The Competitors__c field is unknown. There is no mention in Next Step of the competitor's identity, their differentiators, or how the buyer is comparing the two vendors. The on-site session (2 Mar), BAFO (24 Mar), and PwC reference call (18 May) suggest a head-to-head comparison, but without knowing the competitor Zycus cannot position its differentiation (Merlin AI, integration depth, services model) or defend its pricing. The decision delay (from mid-June to early-to-mid-July, attributed to internal agendas and vacations per Next Step 24 Jun) may also indicate the buyer is negotiating with both finalists in parallel. The next move is to ask the partner (BCT) to name the other finalist and share what the buyer has said about the competitive comparison, so Zycus can adjust its positioning or pricing strategy before the decision is announced.

sources: SF Products: iContract + Merlin (contract management + AI)
SF Next_Step__c: no mention of buyer's pain or problem statement
Gap: pain not documented; inferred from product scope but not confirmed
identify_pain.narrative: The operational or financial pain the buyer is solving is not documented. The products in scope are iContract + Merlin, indicating the buyer needs contract lifecycle management and AI-driven contract intelligence, but the specific pain (manual contract review cycles, compliance risk, spend leakage, lack of visibility into contract obligations, etc.) has never been captured in Salesforce or Next Step. The RFP was received 10 Nov 2025, so the buyer's requirements document likely detailed the pain, but that content was not logged. The on-site session (2 Mar 2026) and the iterative pricing discussions (user definitions, BAFO) suggest the buyer is serious, but without the pain narrative it is impossible to anchor the value case or defend pricing against a lower-cost competitor. The partner (BCT) knows the pain, but Zycus does not have it on record. The next move is to ask the partner to summarize the buyer's stated pain and the cost or risk they are trying to eliminate, so the deal team can reinforce that message if pricing becomes a negotiation point.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: MSA completed with BCT 24 Apr, submitted to IDB, no feedback since 5 May
SF Next_Step__c: BAFO delivered 24 Mar, user definitions shared for refined quote
Gap: no mention of InfoSec review, ERP integration, or current MSA status
paper_process.narrative: The contracting mechanics are in progress but status is unclear. The MSA was completed between Zycus and BCT by 24 April 2026 (Next Step 24 Apr) and submitted to IDB for feedback. As of 5 May 2026, the partner (BCT) was quiet and Harshad was following up for feedback from the buyer (Next Step 5 May). Since then, no update on whether the MSA is agreed, being redlined, or awaiting signature. The BAFO pricing was delivered 24 March 2026, and user definitions were shared to refine the quote (Next Step 2 Mar, 6 Mar). The partner (BCT) is leading implementation, and the MSA alignment between Zycus, BCT, and the buyer is a three-party paper process. InfoSec review and ERP/systems integration scope are not mentioned. The deal is 7 days from close, but the MSA status silence since early May is a blocker risk. The next move is to ask the partner for an urgent update on MSA feedback from the buyer and whether legal/procurement have cleared it for signature.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: no EB, DM, or Exec Sponsor role
SF Next_Step__c: Mariana mentioned 15 Jun and 24 Jun, title and authority unknown
Inference: BCT partner has been the buyer interface (on-site, demo, MSA, reference coordination per Next Step)
economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is unmapped. No EB, Executive Sponsor, or Decision Maker contact role exists in Salesforce. Mariana is referenced in Next Step (15 Jun: check in with Mariana on decision timing; 24 Jun: positive message from IDB but decision delayed), but her title, authority, and budget control are unknown. The partner (BCT) has been the primary interface with the buyer throughout the cycle (led demo, coordinated on-site, aligned MSA, coordinated reference call), so BCT may have EB access, but Zycus does not have a direct relationship. At 7 days to close, the lack of direct EB engagement and the absence of any documented commercial or pricing conversation with a named budget holder is a fatal risk. If the decision is made by someone Zycus has never met, a late 'no' or a demand for a steep discount is likely. The next move is to ask the partner to name the EB and their role in the final decision, and to request an executive connect before the decision is announced.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: full process timeline Nov 2025 to Jun 2026
Inference: committee decision with multiple approvers (delay due to internal agendas)
Gap: approval chain and final decision-maker roles not documented
decision_process.narrative: The decision process the buyer is running is a formal, multi-stage vendor evaluation led by procurement or a sourcing committee, with BCT as the implementation partner. The process ran: RFP round (Nov 2025), vendor demos (Jan 2026), downselection to 2 finalists (Dec 2025/Jan 2026), on-site finalist session (2 Mar 2026), BAFO (24 Mar), MSA review (April/May), reference validation (18 May), and final decision (originally mid-June, delayed to early-to-mid-July per 24 Jun Next Step). The delay is attributed to 'internal agendas and team vacations' (Next Step 24 Jun), indicating a committee decision requiring multiple approvers. Mariana appears to be coordinating the timeline (Next Step 15 Jun, 24 Jun), but her role (sourcing lead, procurement, or a functional buyer) is unknown. The approval chain and whether an executive or CFO must sign off are not documented. The process is methodical and buyer-controlled; Zycus and BCT are responsive, not driving. The next move is to confirm with the partner who the final approvers are and whether the decision will be announced before the 14 Jul close date or will slip.

sources: SF Next_Step__c: RFP, demo, on-site, BAFO, reference call sequence Nov 2025 to May 2026
SF Products: iContract + Merlin (Merlin = AI capability in scope)
Inference: criteria include functional fit, implementation/services, credibility, AI capability
decision_criteria.narrative: The decision criteria are inferred from the process the buyer ran, but not explicitly documented. The buyer issued an RFP (received 10 Nov 2025, submitted 24 Nov), conducted vendor demos (Jan 2026), downselected to finalists (30 Dec 2025, Zycus confirmed as 1 of 2 finalists 11 May 2026), requested an on-site session (2 Mar 2026), asked for updated user counts and refined pricing (6 Mar, 13 Mar), requested a BAFO (delivered 24 Mar), and requested a reference call with PwC (18 May). This sequence indicates a structured evaluation with criteria around functional fit (demo/on-site), services/implementation (pricing iterations, BCT partner involvement), and credibility (PwC reference). The products in scope are iContract + Merlin, so AI/Merlin capability is presumably a criterion. However, the buyer's explicit evaluation scorecard, weighting, and how Zycus ranked against the other finalist are not documented. The next move is to ask the partner for the buyer's decision scorecard and how Zycus is positioned on each criterion relative to the competitor.

items.name: Close date pushed again (Jun 25 → Jul 14)
items.summary: Second consecutive slip in just over a week, with no activity logged to explain it — a sign the deal may be losing momentum internally or with the buyer.

day_summary.overall: No new engagement today — just another close date push, this time from June 25 to July 14. Coming right on the heels of a prior slip from June 18 to June 25, this is the second consecutive delay with no accompanying activity, suggesting the deal is stalling rather than progressing. We're not waiting on any specific deliverable from IDB Invest; the date is simply being rolled forward, which is a signal worth checking on rather than taking at face value.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 54 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Close date in 7d with signature not yet secured. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 44 is carried by differentiation strength +0.30 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 46 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 46 (roughly flat): no activity instrumentation on this record yet (cadence reads fully after its next sweep) — Salesforce shows activity 7d ago. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 5 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 40 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 2 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (44), nudged by commitment 40 and momentum 46, and dragged by risk 54.

contributions.evidence: Win capped at 52: access to power (economic buyer) not established (gap) — a higher win probability has to be earned by qualifying this first

reasons.detail: IDB Invest is a forecasted Upside Key Deal ($100k, Shortlisted, win 45) that is drifting: the close date moved from Jun 18 to Jun 25 on Jun 22, then again from Jun 25 to Jul 14 on Jun 29 - a 19-day slip logged just 4 days ago. The buyer deferred its decision to 'early to mid-July' on internal agendas/team vacations, and this is a 2-finalist CLM bake-off with no confirmed IDB champion and no identified Economic Buyer after 7.5 months. If the slide continues the forecasted commit is at risk and the unnamed rival finalist can close the gap while we have no direct buyer line (all access runs through partner BCT).
reasons.metric: Close date pushed 19 days (Jun 25 -> Jul 14) on Jun 29; win 45, no champion after 7.5 mo
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Grace why IDB's close date slipped again to Jul 14, who our actual internal champion is beyond Mariana, and how she is forcing BCT to name the other finalist and IDB's decision criteria this week.
reasons.summary: Forecasted $100k IDB deal slipping - close date pushed 19 days on Jun 29
reasons.evidence: recent_movements_14d: CloseDate 2026-06-25 -> 2026-07-14, at 2026-06-29 by Grace Kim; next_step 6/24/26: decision update 'early to mid-July' due to internal agendas/team vacations.

confidence_signals.summary: Mixed. Positive: finalist status (11 May), positive message from buyer (24 Jun), PwC reference call completed (18 May), partner (BCT) actively engaged. Negative: no Zycus-buyer engagement since 2 Mar on-site, EB unmapped, competitor unknown, MSA silent for 2 months, decision delayed. Partner-led motion limits Zycus visibility and control.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 7 (close date 2026-07-14, today 2026-07-07). Time in stage: 239 days in Shortlisted since downselection 30 Dec 2025. The decision was originally expected mid-June, then delayed to early-to-mid-July per 24 Jun Next Step. With 7 days to close and no decision announced, a slip is likely. The forward pace required is 0 days (decision must happen NOW), but the buyer's internal agendas and team vacations have created uncontrolled latency. The deal has been in Shortlisted for 8 months, which is long but explicable by the partner-led motion and multi-round finalist evaluation (on-site 2 Mar, BAFO 24 Mar, MSA alignment through April, PwC reference call 18 May). However, the close date is now critically exposed.
evidence: Close date 2026-07-14 is 7 days away with no decision announced (Next Step 24 Jun: decision delayed to early-to-mid-July)
Upside Key Deal forecast is indefensible: no EB mapped, no direct buyer engagement documented since on-site 2 Mar 2026, decision timeline controlled by buyer's internal agendas with no Zycus leverage
Unknown competitor (1 of 2 finalists per Next Step 11 May 2026): cannot position differentiation or defend pricing blind
MSA status unclear since 5 May 2026 (Next Step: quiet from partner on feedback from buyer)
Partner (BCT) is the primary buyer interface; Zycus has no direct champion or EB access documented
north_star_verdict.headline: 7 days to close with decision delayed to early-to-mid-July; competing blind against 1 of 2 finalists with no EB access

flags: Unknown competitor is a high-priority intelligence gap. IDB is comparing two finalists, and we do not know who we are up against or what their edge is (product fit, pricing, brand). Work with BCT immediately to identify the other vendor and IDB's comparison criteria, then position Zycus accordingly (AI/Merlin differentiation, PwC reference strength, ease of use). This is the single highest-leverage move before the 14 July decision.

Economic buyer is unmapped. No EB, Executive Sponsor, or Decision Maker is logged as a contact role, and the Next Step trail does not name the person who approved the $100k budget or will sign the contract. 'Mariana' is referenced as a project coordinator, but her authority is unknown. Partner-led deals often have EB blind spots, but at 7 days to close, Zycus should confirm with BCT who the signatory is and whether they are on board. An unmapped EB at this stage risks a late 'no' from someone we have never engaged.

No quantified ROI or value case is documented. IDB issued an RFP for CLM modernization, but the specific pain (cycle time, compliance risk, manual workload) and the quantified savings (FTE hours, contract approval speed, risk reduction) are not captured. Without a business case, it is difficult to defend pricing or urgency if the other finalist undercuts us. Recommend: extract the value case from the RFP or on-site session notes, or co-build a simple ROI model with IDB/BCT before the final decision (e.g. contract cycle time reduction x contract volume = X hours saved per year).
Economic Buyer unmapped: no EB, DM, or Exec Sponsor contact role; Mariana referenced but authority unknown
Unknown competitor (1 of 2 finalists): cannot position differentiation or defend pricing
No documented ROI or quantified value case to defend pricing
Partner-led motion: BCT is primary buyer interface; Zycus has no direct champion or EB access

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin is in scope (Products: iContract;Merlin), indicating the buyer values AI-driven contract intelligence. However, no calls or Next Step entries document the buyer's AI appetite, Merlin demo depth, or competitive AI positioning. AIS fields are empty. The partner (BCT) led the demo and on-site, so Merlin positioning may have occurred but was not captured. At 7 days to close with an unknown competitor, it is unclear whether Merlin is the wedge or a checkbox.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer expectations are met on functional fit (downselected to finalist, on-site session positive, PwC reference call completed), but commercial and timeline expectations are uncertain. Decision delayed due to buyer's internal agendas; MSA status unclear since early May.

hard.ais_why:
• There are no Avoma calls on this opportunity (zero hits across the opp, account, and idbinvest.org attendee search), so no buyer-stated AI evidence exists. The Salesforce record describes a partner-led (BCT) CLM cycle following a standard RFP, which means a system integrator is running the process around functional CLM scope, not a buyer-led AI evaluation.
• A CLM-only opportunity, partner-driven, with no Merlin, ANA, or Agentic AI product in scope (just iContract and Merlin) and no AI rating or AI commentary captured in the record, is the canonical AI Resistant profile in this framework.
• Without explicit AI champion, POC, or budget evidence, the score holds at the floor. Confidence is Low because there are no live signals at all, and any direct buyer engagement (an Avoma call, written RFP response, or updated rep note) surfacing an AI requirement could move this.
